---
title: "Environmental Geography & Biogeography"
subtitle: "Human-environment interactions, ecosystems, biogeography, pedology, and contemporary environmental challenges."
author: "Geography Team"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

{{< include environmental/00-syllabus.md >}}

---

Welcome to the **Environmental Geography & Biogeography** module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course bridges physical and human geography by examining the complex interactions between human societies and the natural environment — from ecosystem dynamics and biogeography to soil science and contemporary environmental crises.

---

## Part A: Common Topics (NEP-2020 & UGC NET)

These topics are covered in both the NEP-2020 undergraduate syllabus and the UGC NET syllabus. The content below highlights syllabus-specific divisions for each topic.

{{< include environmental/01-environment-concept-types-characteristics.md >}}

{{< include environmental/02-ecosystem-structure-functions.md >}}

{{< include environmental/03-energy-flow-in-ecosystem.md >}}

{{< include environmental/04-biogeochemical-cycles.md >}}

{{< include environmental/05-biomes-concept-types.md >}}

{{< include environmental/06-biodiversity-and-depletion.md >}}

{{< include environmental/07-environmental-hazards-pollution.md >}}

{{< include environmental/08-conservation-management-ecosystems.md >}}

---

## Part B: NEP-2020 Specific Topics

These topics are part of the NEP-2020 undergraduate programme only.

### Biogeography (Unit II)

{{< include environmental/09-environmental-controls-tolerance.md >}}

{{< include environmental/10-biogeography-nature-scope.md >}}

{{< include environmental/11-ecological-succession.md >}}

{{< include environmental/12-biogeographic-processes.md >}}

{{< include environmental/13-biogeographical-regions.md >}}

### Pedology — Soil Geography (Unit III)

{{< include environmental/14-soil-colour-texture-structure.md >}}

{{< include environmental/15-soil-horizons.md >}}

{{< include environmental/16-soil-forming-processes-factors.md >}}

{{< include environmental/17-soil-classification.md >}}

---

## Part C: UGC NET Specific Topics

These topics are part of the UGC NET syllabus only.

### Bio-Geography

{{< include environmental/18-physical-factors-plant-animal-distribution.md >}}

### Human Ecology & Environmental Ethics

{{< include environmental/19-human-ecology.md >}}

{{< include environmental/20-environmental-ethics-deep-ecology.md >}}

### Environmental Hazards & Disasters

{{< include environmental/21-ozone-depletion.md >}}

{{< include environmental/22-global-warming-climate-change.md >}}

{{< include environmental/23-urban-heat-island.md >}}

### Environmental Policy & Governance

{{< include environmental/24-national-programmes-policies.md >}}

{{< include environmental/25-international-treaties-agreements.md >}}

---

### Books and Further Readings

Environmental Geography provides the essential bridge between physical processes and human impacts. Explore [Climatology](climatology.html) and [Oceanography](oceanography.html) for deeper physical science perspectives.



---

## Quick Reference

{{< include environmental/27-quick-reference.md >}}

---
